claim: TableTennisDaily Academy's '5 Steps To Master The Reverse Pendulum Serve' (Oct 2019, ~798k views, 8:16) is the canonical step-progression tutorial; framed as Timo Boll/Fan Zhendong's weapon broken into a learnable progression.
quote: Many professional players such as Timo Boll and Fan Zhendong use this serve to devastating effect, so we've created this step by step guide so you can master it too!

claim: The 5-step pattern: (1) wrist-only feel square to the table, (2) add forearm side-on with elbow lifted, (3) layer waist/shoulder rotation, (4) vary contact point to switch between sidespin/backspin and sidespin/topspin, (5) drill solo.
quote: Cut under the ball more for backspin and brush around the side more to create more topspin... Bring in the forearm for stability and control... Elevate your elbow to create adequate space for the backswing.

claim: MLFM Table Tennis 'Learning KILLER Reverse Pendulum Serve' (Nov 2019, ~315k views, 8:30) is a popular alternative walkthrough emphasising the deadly-spin angle for amateurs.
quote: Learning KILLER Reverse Pendulum Serve | MLFM Table Tennis Tutorial

claim: PingSkills' 'Learn the Backspin Reverse Pendulum Serve' (~2.57M views) is a focused short on the hardest variation — getting genuine backspin out of the reverse motion.
quote: Learn the Backspin Reverse Pendulum Serve | Table Tennis | PingSkills

claim: PingSkills' classic 'Pendulum & Reverse Pendulum Serve' demo (Apr 2008, ~797k views) shows both serves side-by-side, useful as a visual contrast for a player upgrading from pendulum.
quote: Footage from our original Table Tennis DVD showing the pendulum serve and the reverse pendulum serve.

claim: Tom Lodziak hosts coach Tyla Anderson teaching the reverse pendulum (technique, grip, spin variation, service length and placement) plus a return-challenge segment with Tom.
quote: technique, grip, spin variation, service length and service placement... hugely effective but can be tricky to execute

claim: Ti Long Club's 'Zhang Jike's Reverse Pendulum Serve | Tutorial & Secret' (Jun 2021, ~160k views, 6:57) is a strong pro-player breakdown of the most-imitated reverse pendulum action in the modern game.
quote: Ti Long instructs Zhang Jike's Reverse Pendulum Serve technique to the world table tennis community. The most detailed instructions, the easiest to practice.

claim: PingSunday's 'Why Ma Long has very good pendulum serve' (Nov 2022, ~76k views, 5:36) explains why Ma Long's serve sets up his 3rd-ball attack — a strategic 'watch the pros' companion to the technique tutorials.
quote: Ma Long's pendulum serve is short, so it's hard to attack it strong. Because it has sidespin, when you push it, Ma Long will attack it right after the serve (3rd ball attack).

claim: EmRatThich (PingSunday) summarises the reverse pendulum as racket-moves-outside-inward generating right-to-left sidespin; spin direction varies by racket angle and contact point — heavily used by Zhang Jike.
quote: The racket moves from the outside of your body inward during contact... heavily used by professional players like Zhang Jike

claim: 'Zhang Jike Reverse Pendulum Serve Slow Motion' (Aug 2016, ~65k views, 5:06) is a pure slow-motion reference of the canonical pro action, useful for visual modelling without verbal instruction.
quote: Zhang Jike Reverse Pendulum Serve Slow Motion

claim: Inspire Table Tennis's 'Reverse Pendulum Serve Spin Guide [2 Spins, 1 motion]' (Feb 2026, 4:51) targets the deception layer — making backspin and topspin look identical from one motion.
quote: how to create sidespin backspin and sidespin topspin / upspin with 1 motion

claim: JOOLA's Matt Hetherington offers a competing 3-step text framework — rocking-step weight transfer, draw racket into a 'box' with wrist parallel to bicep, flick out of the box into contact — useful as a written supplement.
quote: flick out of the box shape with your wrist and into the ball
